plan.title: Port the slice:* family (record-existing / list) to TypeScript

narratives.Description: Port the scripts/slice_record.py family (slice:record-existing and slice:list) to TypeScript, preserving the slices.jsonl cohort-record shape and the record-existing idempotency contract. This story is independently buildable because the slice module is self-contained, reads and writes only the cohort-record file, and ships a golden parity binary against the Python oracle.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Create the packages/core/src/slice module that ports record-existing and list over the slices.jsonl cohort-record file, preserving field order and idempotency, with vitest unit tests in the module directory.
2. Add a packages/cli/src/slice-parity.ts parity binary that runs the TS verbs and the Python scripts/slice_record.py oracle and asserts byte-identical output with cache off.
3. Verify the port with the new vitest tests and the parity binary while the Python module remains the authoritative oracle.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive maintainer, I want the slice:* cohort-record commands available in TypeScript, so that recording and listing umbrella cohort slices no longer depends on the Python slice_record.py module.

narrative.Acceptance: Running slice:list on the TS port returns the recorded cohort entries in the same slices.jsonl shape the Python module produces.
Running slice:record-existing twice on a matching umbrella and child set persists exactly one cohort record, preserving idempotency.
Running the slice parity binary returns exit 0 and renders byte-identical output to the Python slice_record.py oracle with cache off.

expected_outputs: New packages/core/src/slice module with vitest unit tests
Byte-identical parity vs scripts/slice_record.py under cache-off
swarm.missing_traces_justification: #1530 Wave-3 framework-internal TS port traced to GitHub issue #1727 and umbrella epic #1530; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies to maintainer tooling.

references.title: Issue #1727: feat(ts-migration): port the slice:* family to TS (Wave 3)
